programmes.show_description: Amy Gledhill, Armando Iannucci, Joanna Page, Joel Dommett and Kumail Nanjiani leave all dignity at the door as they hunt for worms and feed Greg his five-a-day.
Cambridge Hotel: Gordon visits Cambridge, NY, where ex-military man John Imhof runs the Cambridge Hotel like a dictatorship, alienating his staff and guests.
In Birmingham, there are pet rats, a festival tent and a shock revelation, as Amanda and Anthony, Steve and Sean, and Oli and LC compete to host the best dinner party.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: George helps a couple turn their dubiously designed, Artex-abound Surrey house into an elegant family home, and tackles a house that's stuck in a wallpaper time warp.
Steve and Crispin prepare their sheep for a speed shearing competition on Dartmoor. In Looe, everyone gears up for the big one-mile wild swim from St George's Island back to the town beach.
The Changing of the Guardian: After surviving a tornado, Marge and Homer seek out guardians for the kids, in case the worst should ever happen to them.
The Day the Earth Stood Cool: Homer persuades a hipster couple to move in next door, but their friendship soon starts to deteriorate...
To Cur with Love: Homer loses Santa's Little Helper but doesn't seem to care, until Grampa tells the story of Homer's long-lost childhood pup Bongo.
Homer Goes to Prep School: Homer joins an off-the-grid survivalist group who have a top-secret doomsday bunker.
A Test Before Trying: When the school's threatened with closure because of low test scores, its fate rests with one student who missed the exam: Bart.
Patrons Ain't: When they realise they don't give much to charity, Doug and Carrie make a donation to the local library. But the library doesn't realise how much it's received.
Eddie Money: Against Carrie's wishes, Doug takes $100 from the 'emergency fund jar' to bet on a boxing match.
Two Thirty: When Doug has a toothache and winds up needing root canal work, he finds out that Carrie used to have a crush on his dentist.
The Dog: Ray and Robert both want to keep a stray bulldog that Ray brings home. But it turns out that the canine isn't so stray after all...
Neighbors: Ray's neighbours demand a meeting at his house to discuss what to do about noisy Frank and interfering Marie. But Frank and Marie gatecrash the gathering.
Fascinatin' Debra: A psychotherapist interviews Debra for an article on the American family but finds the rest of the Barones more interesting than her.
The Two Hundredth Episode: Frasier celebrates his 200th radio broadcast and has tapes of every show - except one. Can any of his obsessive fans help? Bill Gates guest stars.
Sharing Kirby: Frasier and Niles befriend Kirby when they learn that his girlfriend's grandfather is a well-known wine connoisseur.
Felicity Montagu talks about the new series of Beyond Paradise. Francis Bourgeois chats about his new documentary. Plus: Leigh Francis, Jill Francis, Fra Fee and Jordan Rakei.
The Changing of the Guardian: After surviving a tornado, Marge and Homer seek out guardians for the kids, in case the worst should ever happen to them.
Love Is a Many-Splintered Thing: Bart's heartstrings are pulled once again when Mary Spuckler returns to Springfield, but he doesn't pay her enough attention.
Hardly Kirk-ing: Bart and Milhouse explore the perks of adulthood when a hair-shaving experiment leaves Milhouse looking exactly like his dad Kirk.
George Clarke meets a master craftsman who's rescuing a dilapidated Victorian circus wagon, and a man with an ambitious plan to turn a grain silo into a hip, rustic bar.

programmes.show_name: Hamleys: Top 100 Toys of All Time
programmes.show_description: Hamleys opens its doors for a celeb-packed celebration of the toys that defined generations. From timeless classics to modern icons, we explore the biggest toy crazes of the last 200 years.
(2020) Touching Disney Pixar life-after-death-and-before-life animated adventure with Jamie Foxx and Tina Fey. A dead music teacher tries to coax a reluctant soul into a body.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
by Brick: Behind the scenes as one of the UK's busiest theme parks takes adventure to another level with high-octane stunts, intricate model making, ride upgrades and even sharks!
...Off for Stand Up To Cancer: Emmett J Scanlan, Mark Wright, Nella Rose, Mutya Buena and Ralf Little make vegan fruit tarts and recreate their most ridiculous purchase ever.
Alison Hammond hosts the new series giving extraordinary people a chance to perform the one song that tells their story. In Liverpool, a teen sings Bocelli for his foster mum. (Ep1)
The Goggleboxers settle in for I'm a Celebrity... South Africa, Britain's Got Talent, The Dog House, The Apprentice, Secret Garden and the news on the Artemis II moon mission.
Sparks fly as First Dates returns. A former Lioness meets a cat lover. A yoga teacher contemplates a mindfulness coach. And a doctor calms her nerves with a self-confessed geek.
(2019) Raucous teen comedy. A group of Catholic school girls hit Edinburgh for a choir competition, but they're more interested in debauchery than singing.
J Willy's Bar & Grille: Gordon's got his work cut out at J Willy's in Indiana - most of the food is from a can or the freezer, and they're a million dollars in debt.
Bart's Not Dead: Bart takes a dare that lands him in a coma. To get out of trouble, he says he went to heaven and saw Jesus.
Love Is a Many-Splintered Thing: Bart's heartstrings are pulled once again when Mary Spuckler returns to Springfield, but he doesn't pay her enough attention.
Hardly Kirk-ing: Bart and Milhouse explore the perks of adulthood when a hair-shaving experiment leaves Milhouse looking exactly like his dad Kirk.
On Merseyside, Sandra dishes out orders to her husband Graham, Joanne and Simon serve too much spice, and Helen and Adam plan a Michelin star-inspired night of fine dining.

programmes.show_name: George Clarke's Old House New Home
programmes.show_description: In Kent, Pete and Amelie tackle a mammoth Victorian house. In Warwickshire, George helps Michele finish the remodelling of her adjoining Grade ll listed cottages.
Another chance to catch Friday's new episode, with Levi Roots in Dictionary Corner. Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host.
And Coachie Makes Three: Coach keeps hanging around with Sam and Diane, but they get tired of him playing gooseberry and decide to set him up on a date.
Cliff's Rocky Moment: Cliff's constant know-it-all chatter annoys an aggressive customer at the bar, who asks him to step outside for a fight. But is Cliff up to the challenge?
Fortune and Men's Weight: A combined weighing scale/fortune-telling machine proves uncannily prescient when it predicts the future for Norm and Vera, and Sam and Diane.
Why Are We Here?: Ray and Debra reminisce about more peaceful times in their first apartment and recall why they ended up living across the road from Ray's meddlesome parents.
Ray's on TV: Ray's family reluctantly tell him the truth about his performance as a panellist on TV.
Father Knows Least: Ray reluctantly accompanies Debra to parenting classes but then discovers that he can put the skills he learns to good use... with his own parents.
Junior Agent: Now that Frasier's agent is representing a hot new radio psychiatrist, she passes Frasier off to her assistant.
Bully for Martin: Frasier offers his unsolicited help when Martin's boss starts acting like a bully. Roz talks incessantly about her new love, Roger.
Mother Load (Part 1/2): Just as Niles and Daphne agree to move in together, they're visited by her disruptive, conniving mother and lecherous, leeching brother.
Mother Load (Part 2/2): After Frasier boots Daphne's unpleasant brother out of his home, Daphne frets that she'll be left alone to deal with her horrendous mother.
Levanti's: Gordon visits Pennsylvania, where he meets siblings whose inability to work together is risking their father's life savings at his Italian restaurant, Levanti's.
Channel 4's midday news summary.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Buckinghamshire's Chiltern Hills, Henry Cole uncovers an anvil and Simon O'Brien finds an old dresser, which he thinks could become a wildlife sanctuary for birds and insects.

programmes.show_name: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum & Dad
programmes.show_description: The Bakers head to the World Heritage listed Durham Cathedral. Matt goes on an underwater treasure hunt and the whole family try out as choristers.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Ranvir Singh in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Debra and Greg look for a holiday home in the beautiful countryside of Murcia, which one day they hope to make their permanent home. Jasmine Harman shows them what their £150k can buy.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun: Where...
programmes.show_description: ...Are They Now?: We revisit Adam and Alicia at their cocktail bar on the Costa del Sol. Coach tour operator Leslie trials new Spanish culinary experiences.
The new series continues. Can Kathryn and Steve revive their kitchen without losing its sense of history, and their minds?! Sarah and George face their first wedding of the season.
...the Sun: With just a month left until their deadline, Jasmine takes matters - and a sledgehammer - into her own hands. And a family BBQ eases the stresses... but not for long! (Ep11)
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
The kennel takes a shot on Bulut, a stray Malinois who showed up in the night. Can Bailey the cocker spaniel meet expectations? And is it second time lucky for Moose the Staffy?
The Cold Case Murder: The case of the brutal murder of 86-year-old Una Crown in 2013 is re-opened. Police think they know the killer but will the science back them up? (Ep1)
Within hours of Jay Slater's disappearance in the summer of 2024, mass online interest set conspiracy theories flying. This is the exclusive story of Jay's death and its extreme aftermath.
